location: Mount Feilai, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China
historical_period: Founded in 326 AD during the Eastern Jin Dynasty
cultural_significance: Lingyin Temple is one of the oldest and most famous Buddhist temples in China, also known as Temple of Soul's Retreat. It houses numerous ancient statues and artifacts.
highlights: Feilai Feng - Rocky hill with 500 Arhat statues
Mahavira Hall - Main hall with massive Buddha statue
Feilai Feng Grottoes - Over 300 rock carvings from the 10th-14th centuries

history.event: Founded by Indian monk Hui Neng
One of the ten most famous temples in China
Rebuilt and opened as a tourist attraction

religious_significance: The temple has been an important center for Buddhist practice and scholarship throughout Chinese history, attracting monks from all over the country.

visiting_info.highlights: Feilai Feng grottoes, Feilai Feng peak climbing
